key_insight: A PDE-constrained optimization framework can reconstruct both full-field displacement and applied force from sparse sensor data by enforcing the structural dynamics PDE during the recovery process.
engineering_relevance: This enables higher-fidelity virtual sensing for structural health monitoring in complex components where direct full-field measurement is impractical.
method: Treat the elastodynamic equation as an equality constraint.
Optimize the unknown force field with Tikhonov regularization using L-BFGS.
Compute gradients by reverse-mode automatic differentiation through the forward FEM solver, with offline FEniCSx assembly and online GPU-accelerated JAX execution.
evidence_signal: Across a cantilever plate, elbow pipe, and reactor pressure vessel case, the method outperformed modal expansion, cutting displacement error by about 2 to 17× and achieving sub-percent accuracy, with up to 64.8× GPU speedup over CPU.
watch_item: The approach depends on accurate PDE models and can be computationally heavier than modal methods, so performance may degrade under strong modeling error or limited GPU resources.
paper_title: PDE-constrained optimization for virtual sensing in structural dynamics: Full-field displacement and force recovery from sparse sensors
